title: Micron predicts that cars will need 300GB of RAM memory-laden vehicles could exacerbate shortages but create 'robust long-term growth in automotive memory demand'
summary: Micron CEO Sanjay Mehrotra predicts self-driving vehicles will need 300GB of RAM, potentially exacerbating memory shortages. This increased demand could lead to robust long-term growth in automotive memory demand. The complexity of AI supercomputers in vehicles drives this prediction.
insight: AI-powered cars fuel soaring memory chip demand

title: Elon Musk unveils $20 billion TeraFab chip project to make chips, memory, and package processors all under one roof targets a terawatt of annual compute
summary: Elon Musk unveils TeraFab project, a $20 billion chip factory in Texas, to produce chips, memory, and packaged processors under one roof, targeting a terawatt of annual compute.
insight: Game-changing chip manufacturing could disrupt global semiconductor industry dominance.

title: GrapheneOS refuses to comply with new age verification laws for operating systems group says it will never require personal information
summary: GrapheneOS refuses to comply with emerging age verification laws for operating systems, stating it will never require personal information from users.
insight: GrapheneOS prioritizes user privacy over regulatory compliance.

title: California scientists 'FlyTrap attack' on DJI drones demonstrated patterned umbrellas lure autonomous drones close enough to be captured or even induced to crash
summary: Researchers at UC Irvine developed a technique using patterned umbrellas to capture or crash DJI drones, exploiting autonomous navigation patterns.
insight: Exploiting AI-driven navigation flaws in autonomous drones for counter-measures

title: Grab the Sapphire Nitro+ Radeon RX 9070 XT for $799.99 comes bundled with 1000W PSU and free copy of Crimson Desert
summary: The Sapphire Nitro+ Radeon RX 9070 XT features AMD's RDNA 4 architecture, offering exceptional 1440p and 4K gaming performance. This graphics card is currently the company's most powerful offering. It comes bundled with a 1000W PSU and a free copy of Crimson Desert.
insight: Unbeatable 4K gaming performance with bundled premium features

title: Two brothers convicted of running ghost gun factory that produced untraceable 3D printed firearms used 3D printers to build gun frames while associates purchased other parts on eBay
summary: Brothers Brandon and Justin Nudelman were convicted of running a ghost gun factory. They used 3D printers to print gun frames while associates bought other parts online. This allowed them to build and sell untraceable weapons.
insight: Ghost guns pose significant threats to public safety and law enforcement.

title: Intel released its first Pentium chip on this day 33 years ago, came packing 3.1 million transistors fifth-gen x86 chip built on an 800nm process
summary: Intel released its first Pentium chip 33 years ago, marking the beginning of a new era in personal computing. The chip featured 3.1 million transistors and was built on an 800nm process, showcasing the company's advancements in semiconductor technology.
insight: Advancement in processor design revolutionized personal computing forever after.

title: Huawei crowdfunds world s first Mesh Crystal Antenna Wi-Fi 7 router stunning glowing ornament also has a shark fin heat exhaust, but is currently a Japan market exclusive
summary: Huawei has released a Wi-Fi 7 router with a unique mesh crystal antenna. This router features a glowing aesthetic and a shark fin heat exhaust. It is currently exclusive to the Japan market.
insight: Huawei's innovative Wi-Fi technology sets a new industry standard.

title: Blacklyte Athena Pro Review: Watch out, Secretlab?
summary: The Blacklyte Athena Pro gaming chair is well-made and comfortable, but its design and features closely resemble Secretlab's Titan model. It offers good support and a smooth gaming experience. However, its originality and value proposition are compromised by its similarity to an existing product.
insight: Lack of originality in design may impact market differentiation.

title: 'Silicon' is a new five-pound art book charting the semiconductor revolution with full-page die shots and commentary 384 page tome is $99 to pre-order now
summary: Silicon is a 384-page art book showcasing the semiconductor revolution with die shots and commentary, available for pre-order at $99.
insight: Revolution in technology showcased in visually stunning coffee table book.

title: City has roads named Tape Drive and Disk Drive from bygone HDD-making era area was once home to the StorageTek empire
summary: Tape Drive and Disk Drive roads in the Louisville/Broomfield area are named after the city's history with StorageTek, a legendary HDD manufacturer from the bygone era. StorageTek operated in the area and played a significant role in shaping the region's identity. The roads serve as a nostalgic reminder of the city's past.
insight: Local roads preserve history of a once-thriving HDD industry hub

title: CIX ClawCore Armv9.2 CPU family targets OpenClaw deployments
summary: CIX introduces the ClawCore Armv9.2 CPU family optimized for OpenClaw, with three SKUs catering to high-performance and AI workloads. The lineup includes a powerful model with 12-core CPU and 45 TOPS AI compute, as well as an AI-focused model with 80 TOPS AI compute and hardware security features. Shipping starts in March 2026.
insight: Optimized CPUs enable faster, more efficient OpenClaw deployments

title: ESP32-P4 revision 3.0 gains new power rail, requires new PCB design and firmware
summary: Espressif's ESP32-P4 revision 3.0 introduces a new power rail and requires updated designs.
insight: New power rail improves high-performance digital domain stability and reliability

title: Ohm Lab Neuro N6 Modular STM32N6 AI Vision devkit supports rolling shutter, global shutter, or thermal camera (Crowdfunding)
summary: Ohm Lab Neuro N6 is a compact, modular AI Vision devkit powered by an STMicro STM32N6 microcontroller, offering various camera options and expansion capabilities.
insight: Edge AI processing for real-time vision applications with high performance and flexibility.

title: Modder packs a full Windows gaming PC inside Xbox Series X chassis
summary: A modder has successfully packed a Windows gaming PC into an Xbox Series X case, creating a proof-of-concept hybrid device months before Microsoft's Project Helix. This achievement demonstrates the possibility of running PC titles on next-gen Xbox hardware. The mod showcases an innovative and compact gaming solution.
insight: Envisioning hybrid Xbox/PC gaming beyond Microsoft's Project Helix timeline

title: MSI targets Apple's MacBook Neo with new budget Modern laptops
summary: MSI targets Apple's MacBook Neo with budget Modern laptops offering improved performance and features.
insight: MSI challenges Apple's MacBook Neo with high-performance budget laptops.

title: Patreon rejects "fair use" claims for AI training, calls for creator compensation
summary: Patreon rejects 'fair use' claims for AI training, calls for creator compensation.
insight: AI companies should compensate creators for training data usage.

title: Amazon is trying smartphones again after the Fire Phone flop with Alexa-first "Transformer"
summary: Amazon is reviving its smartphone efforts with the 'Transformer', an Alexa-integrated device.
insight: Amazon aims to unify its services through voice assistants and devices.

title: Tesla Semi is finally going into production, and early drivers are already sold
summary: Tesla's Semi is going into production, with early drivers already sold. The truck's design allows drivers to avoid hazards with ease. A tight bend in Nevada showcased the Semi's capabilities.
insight: Tesla Semi's innovative design enhances driver safety and efficiency.

title: Crimson Desert Optimization: Best Settings for Performance and Visual Quality
summary: Crimson Desert optimization helps achieve best balance of image quality and performance. We examine every setting to find optimal trade-offs. This guide provides expert recommendations for a smooth gaming experience.
insight: Optimizing Crimson Desert settings reduces visual noise and boosts performance significantly
